extraction.one_line_summary: KITTEL Marcel won stage 7 in a photo finish over BOASSON HAGEN Edvald after trailing by half a bike length at 50m to go, winning purely on bike throw timing.
tactical_patterns: Quick-Step's Sabatini lead-out dropped Kittel third wheel at 200m, closer positioning than previous stages enabled late acceleration.
Van Rensburg's 180m pull for Dimension Data positioned Boasson Hagen perfectly, revealing team's lead-out capability without Cavendish structure.
Kittel averaged 42.3mph final 500m starting from further back, suggesting he's deliberately timing acceleration later than standard sprint protocol.
Boasson Hagen chose inside line on right-bending finale but threw bike after line, indicating non-specialist sprinters lack micro-timing calibration.

rider_signals.KITTEL Marcel: positive: Third win from seven stages shows peak form, now winning from disadvantaged positions through superior timing rather than pure power
rider_signals.BOASSON HAGEN Edvald: positive: Nearly won mass sprint without dedicated train, signals Dimension Data has functional lead-out post-Cavendish crash
rider_signals.VAN RENSBURG Jaco: positive: Delivered 180m lead-out for non-sprinter teammate, proving utility as flexible domestique for opportunistic sprint setups
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: neutral: Sky prioritized front positioning in windy technical finale despite GC not under threat, revealing conservative risk management
